mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy stance, which generally supports equity valuations by lowering the discount rate and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative on average when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, translating into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE declines by >0.5pp over 3 months during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with very low interest rates indicate robust consumer demand supported by cheap credit. This environment fosters corporate revenue growth and expands profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met, or the interaction term shows no predictive power (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production in a low inflation environment suggests efficient supply-side expansion without overheating, leading to sustainable economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when INDPRO growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong underlying demand and investment in the real estate sector, which has broad positive spillover effects across the economy, supporting S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month growth shows no statistically significant correlation with SPX 5-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, signals potential central bank tightening or erosion of purchasing power, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive on average when CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with strong retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy consumer base with both the means and willingness to spend, driving corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of positive PAYEMS and RSAFS growth does not lead to significantly positive SPX 20-day returns over a 5-year period (t-statistic < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth, particularly following a downturn, indicates a robust economic recovery that boosts corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods following GDPC1 growth exceeding 3% during post-recession phases.
